title: Visiting Palazzo Pitti & Boboli Gardens Florence: Complete Guide
highlights: commissions.  The Medici dynasty’s extinction in 1737 with Gian Gastone de’ Medici’s death transferred Palazzo Pitti to the Habsburg-Lorraine family under terms negotiated between European powers. The last Medici ruler, Electress Palatine Anna Maria Luisa, secured the “Family Pact” guaranteeing that Medici art collections would remain in Florence as inalienable public patrimony—a visionary stipulation that preserved the palace galleries for future generations.
